title: Olytic Hook Event
description: Canonical schema for Claude hook events that trigger Aule's automated change-propagation and session lifecycle behaviors. Hooks are declared in hooks.json and fire on system lifecycle events.
read_timing: deploy time — hooks.json is loaded when the plugin is activated

PostToolUse.description: Fires after any Write or Edit tool call completes. Used by aule-change-analyzer to detect file changes and route propagation.

payload.tool_name: string — name of the tool that was called
payload.file_path: string — path of the file that was written or edited

UserPromptSubmit.description: Fires when the user submits a prompt. Used for intent detection (e.g., triggering trashbot on 'sweep plugins').

SessionClose.description: Fires when a session ends. Used by aule-session-summarizer to write episodic memory to the vault.

payload.timestamp: string — ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of session close

PreToolUse.description: Fires before a tool is called. Used for pre-execution validation or permission checks.

payload.tool_name: string — name of the tool about to be called
payload.tool_input: object — the input that will be passed to the tool

matcher.description: Regex pattern to match against trigger context (tool name, prompt text, etc.)

prompt.description: Instructions injected into Claude's context when the hook fires

timeout.description: Maximum seconds before the hook is cancelled

decision.description: For UserPromptSubmit hooks: whether to approve, block, or skip the prompt after hook fires

notes: Hooks are declared in the plugin's hooks.json file, referenced by plugin.json via the 'hooks' field
PostToolUse hooks trigger aule-change-analyzer for all Write/Edit operations on plugin source or contracts/schemas/
SessionClose hooks trigger aule-session-summarizer only for plugins with memory_scope=persistent
Hook prompts are injected instructions — they run in Claude's context, not as separate processes
